title: Unauthorized Writes to Modbus Holding Registers

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

spl:
index=ot_protocol sourcetype="edge_hub_modbus" earliest=-24h
| where function_code IN (5,6,15,16,22,23)
| lookup modbus_write_acl.csv src_ip dest_plc OUTPUT role
| where role!="engineer_approved" OR isnull(role)
| stats values(register_start) as regs, count by src_ip, dest_plc, user
| sort - count
description: Detects Modbus function codes that modify process values from hosts not authorized for write operations.
value: Detects Modbus function codes that modify process values from hosts not authorized for write operations.
grandmaExplanation: We catch our sensors function codes that modify process values from hosts not authorized for write operations ‚Äî so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
implementation: (1) Build ACL from approved EWS IPs; (2) segment read-only analytics clients; (3) test with HIL bench; (4) integrate with SOAR for host isolation.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Edge Hub.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `edge_hub_modbus` with `function_code`, `src_ip`.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
(1) Build ACL from approved EWS IPs; (2) segment read-only analytics clients; (3) test with HIL bench; (4) integrate with SOAR for host isolation.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ot_protocol sourcetype="edge_hub_modbus" earliest=-24h
| where function_code IN (5,6,15,16,22,23)
| lookup modbus_write_acl.csv src_ip dest_plc OUTPUT role
| where role!="engineer_approved" OR isnull(role)
| stats values(register_start) as regs, count by src_ip, dest_plc, user
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Unauthorized Writes to Modbus Holding Registers (IEC 62443-3-3 / SR 2.1)** ‚Äî Detects Modbus function codes that modify process values from hosts not authorized for write operations.

Documented **Data sources**: `edge_hub_modbus` with `function_code`, `src_ip`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Edge Hub. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ot_protocol; **sourcetype**: edge_hub_modbus. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=ot_protocol, sourcetype="edge_hub_modbus", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where function_code IN (5,6,15,16,22,23)` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where role!="engineer_approved" OR isnull(role)` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by src_ip, dest_plc, user** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table, Heatmap (IP√óPLC), Time chart.
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern ‚Äî correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
